description: In Go, never hand-edit go generate output and code emitted by stringer/protoc-gen-go; change the source and rerun the generator.
body: In Go, go generate output and code emitted by stringer/protoc-gen-go are derived artifacts: editing them directly drifts from the generator and is overwritten on the next run. Use when a diff touches generated files, codegen inputs, or the the Go toolchain version (go.mod go directive) and module mode that produces them. Do not use this to justify patching generated output by hand or skipping regeneration. Actions: locate the generator and its inputs, edit the source, rerun the generation step, then run go build ./... with the module's build tags and GOOS/GOARCH so the regenerated output is what ships. Validation signals include a clean regeneration diff, a passing go test ./... -race on the changed package, and no manual edits left in the generated tree. Risk boundary: escalate before regenerating if the generator is pinned, vendored, or owned by another team, and roll back if regeneration changes unrelated files.
